summary: PR 2397 made a focused matching fix in ftCo_DownBound.c for fighter common function ftCo_80097D40. The diff refactored shared branch logic into static helper inlineA1, changed Fighter access in the refactored code to GET_FIGHTER(gobj), removed an 8-byte dummy stack local from ftCo_80097D40, and left ftCo_80097D40 calling inlineA1 before clearing fp->mv.co.downspot.x4. The automated report records ftCo_80097D40 as a new 72-byte match from 0.00% to 100.00%, plus a small .sdata2 improvement.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Try helper extraction before dummy stack padding for small function matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2397 matched ftCo_80097D40 by moving the conditional ftCo_80097AF4/ftCo_8009794C call sequence into static inlineA1, calling that helper from ftCo_80097D40, and removing an 8-byte dummy local. This is evidence that helper-boundary/source-shape changes can replace stack-padding locals for some small fighter matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineA1 introduced and called from ftCo_80097D40
raw_diff: u8 _[8] = { 0 } removed
text_corpus: bot report ftCo_80097D40 0.00% to 100.00%, +72 bytes

source_update_candidates.title: Helper extraction as an alternative to stack-padding tactics
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a note under matching tactics or authored-source-style standards: when a small function only matches with dummy stack locals, test whether nearby authored/inlined helper boundaries better explain the assembly before keeping padding locals.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed u8 _[8] dummy local
raw_diff: introduced static inlineA1 and called it from ftCo_80097D40
text_corpus: automated report records ftCo_80097D40 as matched
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR is a small but clear example aligning with existing standards that dummy locals need evidence and likely authored source shape should be preferred.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all direct gobj->user_data accesses should be replaced with GET_FIGHTER; this PR only shows that GET_FIGHTER was part of the successful ftCo_80097D40 shape.
Do not infer that inlineA1 is a semantic or globally preferred helper name; the PR only introduces it locally.
Do not claim human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not promote the .sdata2 report improvement into a data-section lesson because the diff does not explain the mechanism.
